alpha) * latency_ms;\n116\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n120\t    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) }\n143\t}\n144\t\n145\timpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n146\t    fn default() -> Self {\n147\t        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n148\t    }\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t/// Callback for reporting selection    }\n213\t\n214\t    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n215\t    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n216\t        let metrics = self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), score);\n228\t            }\n229\t            return selected;\n230\t        }\n231\t\n232\t        // Compute scores and collect all nodes with the Adaptive replica selection using EWMA scoring (plan §13.3).\n//!\n//! Replaces round-robin with latency-aware selection using EWMA-smoothed\n//! metrics: latency p95, in-flight request count, and error rate.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse rand::prelude::*;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Replica selection strategy.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum SelectionStrategy {\n    /// EWMA-based adaptive selection.\n    Adaptive,\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    RoundRobin,\n    /// Random selection.\n    Random,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SelectionStrategy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::Adaptive\n    }\n}\n\n/// Replica selection configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n    /// Selection strategy.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub strategy: String,\n    /// Latency weight in score computation.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_latency_weight\")]\n    pub latency_weight: f64,\n    /// In-flight request weight.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_inflight_weight\")]\n    pub inflight_weight: f64,\n    /// Error rate weight.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_error_weight\")]\n    pub error_weight: f64,\n    /// EWMA half-life in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_ewma_half_life\")]\n    pub ewma_half_life_ms: u64,\n    /// Exploration epsilon (probability of random selection).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_epsilon\")]\n    pub exploration_epsilon: f64,\n}\n\nfn default_latency_weight() -> f64 {\n    1.0\n}\nfn default_inflight_weight() -> f64 {\n    2.0\n}\nfn default_error_weight() -> f64 {\n    10.0\n}\nfn default_ewma_half_life() -> u64 {\n    5000\n}\nfn default_epsilon() -> f64 {\n    0.05\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strategy: \"adaptive\".into(),\n            latency_weight: default_latency_weight(),\n            inflight_weight: default_inflight_weight(),\n            error_weight: default_error_weight(),\n            ewma_half_life_ms: default_ewma_half_life(),\n            exploration_epsilon: default_epsilon(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Per-node metrics for adaptive selection.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeMetrics {\n    /// EWMA of latency p95 in milliseconds.\n    pub latency_p95_ms: f64,\n    /// Current in-flight request count.\n    pub in_flight: u32,\n    /// EWMA of error rate (0.0 to 1.0).\n    pub error_rate: f64,\n    /// EWMA half-life for updates.\n    pub half_life_ms: u64,\n    /// Last update timestamp.\n    pub last_updated: Instant,\n}\n\nimpl NodeMetrics {\n    /// Create new metrics with initial values.\n    pub fn new(initial_latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            latency_p95_ms: initial_latency_ms,\n            in_flight: 0,\n            error_rate: 0.0,\n            half_life_ms,\n            last_updated: Instant::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update latency with EWMA smoothing.\n    pub fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * self.latency_p95_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * latency_ms;\n        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n        let new_error = if is_error { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };\n        self.error_rate = alpha * self.error_rate + (1.0 - alpha) * new_error;\n        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Increment in-flight count.\n    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the composite score (lower is better).\n    pub fn score(&self, config: &ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> f64 {\n        config.latency_weight * self.latency_p95_ms\n            + config.inflight_weight * (self.in_flight as f64)\n            + config.error_weight * (self.error_rate * 1000.0)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Callback for reporting selection events.\npub trait SelectionObserver: Send + Sync {\n    /// Called when a node is selected with its score.\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64);\n    /// Called when exploration selects a random node.\n    fn report_exploration(&self);\n}\n\n/// No-op observer for when metrics aren't needed.\nstruct NoOpObserver;\nimpl SelectionObserver for NoOpObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, _node_id: &str, _score: f64) {}\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {}\n}\n\n/// Replica selector.\npub struct ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n    /// Per-node metrics.\n    metrics: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<NodeId, NodeMetrics>>>,\n    /// Round-robin counter (for round-robin strategy).\n    rr_counter: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, u64>>>,\n    /// Random number generator.\n    rng: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<StdRng>>,\n    /// Observer for selection events.\n    observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>,\n}\n\nimpl ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Create a new replica selector with a metrics observer.\n    pub fn new_with_observer(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig, observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            metrics: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            rr_counter: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            rng: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(StdRng::from_entropy())),\n            observer,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new replica selector without metrics.\n    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n        Self::new_with_observer(config, Arc::new(NoOpObserver))\n    }\n\n    /// Select a node from the given candidates.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the selected node ID, or None if candidates is empty.\n    pub async fn select(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u32) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let strategy = self.parse_strategy();\n\n        match strategy {\n            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n\n        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n        if self.should_explore() {\n            self.observer.report_exploration();\n            let selected = self.select_random(candidates);\n            if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n                let score = metrics\n                    .get(node)\n                    .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n                    .unwrap_or(1000.0);\n                self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), score);\n            }\n            return selected;\n        }\n\n        // Compute scores and collect all nodes with the minimum score\n        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n        let mut best_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = Vec::new();\n\n        for node in candidates {\n            let score = metrics\n                .get(node)\n                .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n                .unwrap_or(1000.0); // High default for unknown nodes\n\n            if score < best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                best_nodes.clear();\n                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n            } else if (score - best_score).abs() < 1e-10 {\n                // Scores are essentially equal - add to tie list\n                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If multiple nodes have the same best score, pick randomly\n        let selected = if best_nodes.len() == 1 {\n            best_nodes.into_iter().next()\n        } else {\n            let idx = self.rng.lock().unwrap().gen_range(0..best_nodes.len());\n            best_nodes.get(idx).cloned()\n        };\n\n        if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n            self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), best_score);\n        }\n\n        selected\n    }\n\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u64) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        let key = format!(\"group_{}\", group_id);\n        let mut counter = self.rr_counter.write().await;\n        let idx = *counter.entry(key.clone()).or_insert(0) as usize % candidates.len();\n        *counter.get_mut(&key).unwrap() += 1;\n        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n    }\n\n    /// Random selection.\n    fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n        let idx = self\n            .rng\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .gen_range(0..candidates.len());\n        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we should explore (random selection).\n    fn should_explore(&self) -> bool {\n        let mut rng = self.rng.lock().unwrap();\n        rng.gen::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful request (update latency).\n    pub async fn record_success(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: f64) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n        entry.update_error(false);\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed request.\n    pub async fn record_error(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: Option<f64>) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        if let Some(lat) = latency_ms {\n            entry.update_latency(lat);\n        }\n        entry.update_error(true);\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Record that a request is being sent to a node.\n    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, node: &NodeId) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        entry.increment_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Get metrics for a node.\n    pub async fn get_metrics(&self, node: &NodeId) -> Option<NodeMetrics> {\n        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n        metrics.get(node).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the strategy from config string.\n    fn parse_strategy(&self) -> SelectionStrategy {\n        match self.config.strategy.as_str() {\n            \"round_robin\" => SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin,\n            \"random\" => SelectionStrategy::Random,\n            _ => SelectionStrategy::Adaptive,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Clone for ReplicaSelector {\n    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: self.config.clone(),\n            metrics: Arc::clone(&self.metrics),\n            rr_counter: Arc::clone(&self.rr_counter),\n            rng: Arc::clone(&self.rng),\n            observer: Arc::clone(&self.observer),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelector {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n        assert_eq!(config.strategy, \"adaptive\");\n        assert_eq!(config.latency_weight, 1.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.inflight_weight, 2.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.error_weight, 10.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_metrics_score() {\n        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(50.0, 5000);\n        assert_eq!(metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default()), 50.0);\n\n        metrics.in_flight = 5;\n        let score = metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n        // 50 * 1.0 + 5 * 2.0 = 60\n        assert_eq!(score, 60.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_metrics_ewma() {\n        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(100.0, 1000); // Short half-life\n\n        metrics.update_latency(50.0);\n        // Should move toward 50\n        assert!(metrics.latency_p95_ms < 100.0 && metrics.latency_p95_ms > 40.0);\n\n        metrics.update_error(true);\n        assert!(metrics.error_rate > 0.0);\n\n        metrics.update_error(false);\n        // Error rate should decay\n        let rate_before = metrics.error_rate;\n        metrics.update_error(false);\n        assert!(metrics.error_rate < rate_before);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_adaptive() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n\n        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n\n        // Seed metrics by recording successful requests\n        selector.record_success(&node1, 10.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(&node2, 100.0).await;\n\n        // Should select node-1 (lower score)\n        let candidates = vec![node2.clone(), node1.clone()];\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_round_robin() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n            strategy: \"round_robin\".into(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(config);\n\n        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n\n        let candidates = vec![node1.clone(), node2.clone()];\n\n        // First call should return node-1\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1.clone()));\n\n        // Second call should return node-2\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node2.clone()));\n\n        // Third call should wrap to node-1\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_record_request_lifecycle() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n        let node = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n\n        selector.record_request_start(&node).await;\n\n        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 1);\n\n        // Record success decrements in-flight and updates latency\n        selector.record_success(&node, 50.0).await;\n\n        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n        // In-flight should be decremented (from 1 to 0)\n        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_empty_candidates() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n        let selected = selector.select(&[], 0).await;\n        assert!(selected.is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":474,"startLine":1,"totalLines":474}}}

Tests the EWMA-scored replica selection that replaces round-robin:\n4\t//! - score(node) = α · latency_p95_ms + β · in_flight_count + γ · error_rate\n5\t//! - Router picks lowest-scoring eligible node with probability 1 − ε\n6\t//! - With ε (default 0.05) picks uniformly random for exploration\n7\t\n8\tuse miroir_core::replica_selection::{ReplicaSelectionConfig, ReplicaSelector};\n9\tuse ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n15\t}\n16\t\n17\t/// Create a selector with custom half-life for faster test convergence.\n18\tfn fast_test_selector(half_life_ms: u64) -> ReplicaSelector {\n19\t    ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n20\t        ewma_half_life_ms: half_life_ms,\n21\t        exploration_epsilon: 0.05,\n22\t        ..Default::default()\n23\t    })\n24\t}\n25\t\n26\t/// Create a test group with 3 nodes.\n27\tfn test_group_3() -> Group {\n28\t    let mut group = Group::new(0);\n29\t    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".to_string()));\n30\t    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string()));\n31\t    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string()));\n32\t    group\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\t/// Simulate queries to measure node selection distribution.\n36\tasync fn simulate_selections(\n37\t    selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n38\t    candidates: &[NodeId],\n39\t    group_id: u32,\n40\t    count: usize,\n41\t) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> {\n42\t    let mut distribution = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n43\t    for _ in 0..count {\n44\t        if let Some(node) = selector.select(candidates, group_id).await {\n45\t            *distribution.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n46\t        }\n47\t    }\n48\t    distribution\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n52\t// P5.3-A1: Traffic shifts away from degraded node within 2× half-life\n53\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n54\t\n55\t#[tokio::test]\n56\tasync fn p5_3_a1_degraded_node_receives_less_traffic() {\n57\t    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms half-life for fast test\n58\t    let group = test_group_3();\n59\t    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n60\t\n61\t    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n62\t    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n63\t    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n64\t\n65\t    // Initialize all nodes with similar good latency (50ms)\n66\t    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n67\t    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n68\t    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n69\t\n70\t    // Run initial queries to establish baseline\n71\t    let baseline = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n72\t\n73\t    // Verify roughly uniform distribution initially\n74\t    let count0 = *baseline.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n75\t    let count1 = *baseline.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n76\t    let count2 = *baseline.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n77\t\n78\t    // Each node should get ~33% (allow 20-45% for randomness)\n79\t    let _expected = 200 / 3;\n80\t    assert!(\n81\t        (20..=90).contains(&count0),\n82\t        \"node-0 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n83\t        count0\n84\t    );\n85\t    assert!(\n86\t        (20..=90).contains(&count1),\n87\t        \"node-1 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n88\t        count1\n89\t    );\n90\t    assert!(\n91\t        (20..=90).contains(&count2),\n92\t        \"node-2 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n93\t        count2\n94\t    );\n95\t\n96\t    // Induce degradation on node-1: 200ms latency\n97\t *degraded.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n110\t\n111\t    // Node-1 should receive significantly less traffic\n112\t    // With latency_weight=1.0, node-1 score ~200 vs ~50 for others\n113\t    //    );\n119\t\n120\t    // Healthy nodes should receive more traffic\n121\t    assert!(\n122\t        degraded_count0 > 50,\n123\t        \"healthy node-0 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n124\t        degraded_count0\n125\t    );\n126\t    assert!(\n127\t        degraded_count2 > 50,\n128\t        \"healthy node-2 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n129\t        degraded_count2\n130\t    );\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n134\t// P5.3-A2: Node recovers after latency clears; distribution returns to uniform\n135\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n136\t\n137\t#[tokio::test]\n138\tasync fn p5_3_a2_degraded_node_recovers() {\n139\t    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms &candidates[1];\n145\t    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n146\t\n147\t    // Initialize with good latency\n148\t    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n149\t    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n150\t    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n151\t\n152\t    // Degrade node-1 with 200ms latency\n153\t    for _ in 0..10 {\n154\t        selector.record_success(node1,      \"node-1 should be degraded, got {} selections\",\n165\t        degraded_count1\n166\t    );\n167\t\n168\t    // Clear latency: record good responses for node-1\n169\t    for _ in 0..10 {\n170\t        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n171\t    }\n172\t\n173\t    // Wait for recovery (2× half-life)\n174\t    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n175\t\n176\t    //    let recovered_count1 = *recovered.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n181\t    let recovered_count2 = *recovered.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n182\t\n183\t    // All nodes should have roughly equal distribution (~1/3 each)\n184\t    let expected = 300 / 3;\n185\t    let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.4) as usize; // 40% tolerance for exploration\n186\t\n187\t    assert!(\n188\t        (recovered_count1 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n189\t        \"node-1 recovered count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n190\t        recovered_count1, expected, tolerance\n191\t    );\n192\t    assert!(\n193\t        (recovered_count0 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n194\t        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n195\t        recovered_count0, expected, tolerance\n196\t    );\n197\t    assert!(\n198\t        (recovered_count2 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n199\t        \"node-2 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n200\t        recovered_count2, expected, tolerance\n201\t    );\n202\t}\n203\t\n204\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n205\t// P5.3-A3: Exploration ensures degraded node still gets some traffic (ε = 0.05)\n206\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n207\t\n208\t#[tokio::test]\n209\tasync fn p5_3_a3_exploration_samples_degraded_node() {\n210\t    let selector = });\n215\t\n216\t    let group = test_group_3();\n217\t    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n218\t\n219\t    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n220\t    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n221\t    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n222\t\n223\t    // Initialize: node-0 and node-1 healthy, node-2 severely degraded\n224\t    for _ in 0..10 {\n225\t        selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n226\t        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n227\t        selector.record_success(node2, 500.0).await; // Very high latency\n228\t    }\n229\t\n230\t    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n231\t\n232\t    // Run 1000 queries\n233\t    let dist = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 1000).await;\n234\t\n235\t    let count0 = *dist.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n236\t    let count1 = *dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n237\t    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n238\t\n239\t    println!(\"Distribution: node-0={}, node-1={}, node-2={}\", count0, count1, count2);\n240\t\n241\t    // Node-2 is severely degraded but should still get ~5% via exploration\n242\t    // Expected: ~50 selections (5% of 1000), allow range 20-80 for randomness\n243\t    assert!(\n244\t        (20..=80).contains(&count2),\n245\t        \"exploration not working: degraded node-2 got {} selections, expected ~50 (range 20-80)\",\n246\t        count2\n247\t    );\n248\t\n249\t    // Healthy nodes should split the remaining ~95%\n250\t    let healthy_total = count0 + count1;\n251\t    assert!(\n252\t        healthy_total >= 900,\n253\t        \"healthy nodes didn't get enough traffic: {}\",\n254\t        healthy_total\n255\t    );\n256\t\n257\t    // Each healthy node should get roughly half of remaining\n258\t    let expected_healthy = 475; // ~95% / 2\n259\t    let tolerance = 100;\n260\t    assert!(\n261\t        (count0 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n262\t        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n263\t        count0, expected_healthy\n264\t    );\n265\t    assert!(\n266\t        (count1 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n267\t        \"node-1 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n268\t        count1, expected_healthy\n269\t    );\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n273\t// P5.3-A4: Round-robin fallback mode works identically to Phase 1 baseline\n274\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n275\t\n276\t#[tokio::test]\n277\tasync fn p5_3_a4_round_robin_fallback() {\n278\t    let selector = 0.0);\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t#[tokio::test]\n409\tasync fn test_exploration_epsilon_configurable() {\n410\t    // Test with epsilon = 0.0 (no exploration)\n411\t    let selector_no_explore = assert_eq!(\n436\t        count2, 0,\n437\t        \"with epsilon=0.0, degraded node should get zero traffic\"\n438\t    );\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t#[tokio::test]\n442\tasync fn test_random_strategy() {\n443\t    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n444\t        strategy: \"random\".to_string(),\n445\t        ..Default::default()\n446\t    });\n447\t\n448\t    let group = test_group_3();\n449\t    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n450\t\n451\t    // Run many selections to test uniformity\n452\t    let mut counts = *counts.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n456\t        }\n457\t    }\n458\t\n459\t    // Each node should get roughly 1/3 of selections\n460\t    let expected = 1000 / 3;\n461\t    for (node, count) in &counts {\n462\t        let diff = (*count as isize - expected as isize).abs();\n463\t        assert!(\n464\t            diff <= 100, // Allow 10% variance\n465\t            \"{}: got {} selections, expected ~{}\",\n466\t            node, count, expected\n467\t        );\n468\t    }\n469\t}\n470\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c00b8833-b504-45b3-8820-2722c99c2a39","uuid":"f8dce8b7-dd07-42e2-815b-a8ec109fae4a","timestamp":"2026-05-23T17:31:20.706Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/tests/p13_3_adaptive_replica_selection.rs","content":"//! P5.3 §13.3 Adaptive replica selection (EWMA-based) acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the EWMA-scored replica selection that replaces round-robin:\n//! - score(node) = α · latency_p95_ms + β · in_flight_count + γ · error_rate\n//! - Router picks lowest-scoring eligible node with probability 1 − ε\n//! - With ε (default 0.05) picks uniformly random for exploration\n\nuse miroir_core::replica_selection::{ReplicaSelectionConfig, ReplicaSelector};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\n/// Create a test selector with default config.\nfn test_selector() -> ReplicaSelector {\n    ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n}\n\n/// Create a selector with custom half-life for faster test convergence.\nfn fast_test_selector(half_life_ms: u64) -> ReplicaSelector {\n    ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        ewma_half_life_ms: half_life_ms,\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.05,\n        ..Default::default()\n    })\n}\n\n/// Create a test group with 3 nodes.\nfn test_group_3() -> Group {\n    let mut group = Group::new(0);\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".to_string()));\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string()));\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string()));\n    group\n}\n\n/// Simulate queries to measure node selection distribution.\nasync fn simulate_selections(\n    selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n    candidates: &[NodeId],\n    group_id: u32,\n    count: usize,\n) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> {\n    let mut distribution = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    for _ in 0..count {\n        if let Some(node) = selector.select(candidates, group_id).await {\n            *distribution.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n    }\n    distribution\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A1: Traffic shifts away from degraded node within 2× half-life\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a1_degraded_node_receives_less_traffic() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms half-life for fast test\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize all nodes with similar good latency (50ms)\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Run initial queries to establish baseline\n    let baseline = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n\n    // Verify roughly uniform distribution initially\n    let count0 = *baseline.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count1 = *baseline.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count2 = *baseline.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // Each node should get ~33% (allow 20-45% for randomness)\n    let _expected = 200 / 3;\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count0),\n        \"node-0 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count0\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count1),\n        \"node-1 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count1\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count2),\n        \"node-2 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count2\n    );\n\n    // Induce degradation on node-1: 200ms latency\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 200.0).await;\n    }\n\n    // Wait for EWMA to propagate (2× half-life = 200ms)\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run queries after degradation\n    let degraded = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n\n    let degraded_count1 = *degraded.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let degraded_count0 = *degraded.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let degraded_count2 = *degraded.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // Node-1 should receive significantly less traffic\n    // With latency_weight=1.0, node-1 score ~200 vs ~50 for others\n    // Expect node-1 to get <15% of traffic\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count1 < 30,\n        \"degraded node-1 still receiving too much traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count1\n    );\n\n    // Healthy nodes should receive more traffic\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count0 > 50,\n        \"healthy node-0 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count0\n    );\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count2 > 50,\n        \"healthy node-2 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count2\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A2: Node recovers after latency clears; distribution returns to uniform\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a2_degraded_node_recovers() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms half-life\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize with good latency\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Degrade node-1 with 200ms latency\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 200.0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Verify degradation worked\n    let degraded_dist = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 100).await;\n    let degraded_count1 = *degraded_dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count1 < 20,\n        \"node-1 should be degraded, got {} selections\",\n        degraded_count1\n    );\n\n    // Clear latency: record good responses for node-1\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    }\n\n    // Wait for recovery (2× half-life)\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run queries after recovery\n    let recovered = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 300).await;\n\n    let recovered_count0 = *recovered.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let recovered_count1 = *recovered.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let recovered_count2 = *recovered.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // All nodes should have roughly equal distribution (~1/3 each)\n    let expected = 300 / 3;\n    let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.4) as usize; // 40% tolerance for exploration\n\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count1 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-1 recovered count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count1, expected, tolerance\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count0 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count0, expected, tolerance\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count2 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-2 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count2, expected, tolerance\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A3: Exploration ensures degraded node still gets some traffic (ε = 0.05)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a3_exploration_samples_degraded_node() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 100,\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.05, // 5% exploration\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize: node-0 and node-1 healthy, node-2 severely degraded\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(node2, 500.0).await; // Very high latency\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run 1000 queries\n    let dist = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 1000).await;\n\n    let count0 = *dist.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count1 = *dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    println!(\"Distribution: node-0={}, node-1={}, node-2={}\", count0, count1, count2);\n\n    // Node-2 is severely degraded but should still get ~5% via exploration\n    // Expected: ~50 selections (5% of 1000), allow range 20-80 for randomness\n    assert!(\n        (20..=80).contains(&count2),\n        \"exploration not working: degraded node-2 got {} selections, expected ~50 (range 20-80)\",\n        count2\n    );\n\n    // Healthy nodes should split the remaining ~95%\n    let healthy_total = count0 + count1;\n    assert!(\n        healthy_total >= 900,\n        \"healthy nodes didn't get enough traffic: {}\",\n        healthy_total\n    );\n\n    // Each healthy node should get roughly half of remaining\n    let expected_healthy = 475; // ~95% / 2\n    let tolerance = 100;\n    assert!(\n        (count0 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n        count0, expected_healthy\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (count1 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n        \"node-1 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n        count1, expected_healthy\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A4: Round-robin fallback mode works identically to Phase 1 baseline\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a4_round_robin_fallback() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        strategy: \"round_robin\".to_string(),\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 5000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Test round-robin order\n    let first = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let second = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let third = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let fourth = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n\n    // Should cycle through candidates in order\n    assert_eq!(first, candidates.get(0).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(second, candidates.get(1).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(third, candidates.get(2).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(fourth, candidates.get(0).cloned()); // Wrap around\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional tests for EWMA behavior\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn ewma_converges_toward_new_values() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(50);\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let node = &group.nodes()[0];\n\n    // Start with 100ms latency\n    selector.record_success(node, 100.0).await;\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node).await;\n    assert!(metrics1.is_some());\n    let latency1 = metrics1.unwrap().latency_p95_ms;\n    assert!((90.0..=110.0).contains(&latency1));\n\n    // Shift to 50ms\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_success(node, 50.0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n\n    let metrics2 = selector.get_metrics(node).await;\n    let latency2 = metrics2.unwrap().latency_p95_ms;\n\n    // Should move toward 50\n    assert!(latency2 < latency1, \"EWMA should decrease toward new value\");\n    assert!(latency2 > 40.0, \"EWMA shouldn't overshoot too much\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn in_flight_count_affects_score() {\n    let selector = test_selector();\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n\n    // Both have same latency initially\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Add in-flight requests to node-0\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_request_start(node0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n\n    // With inflight_weight=2.0, node-0 score should be higher\n    let metrics0 = selector.get_metrics(node0).await.unwrap();\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node1).await.unwrap();\n\n    let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n    let score0 = metrics0.score(&config);\n    let score1 = metrics1.score(&config);\n\n    assert!(\n        score0 > score1,\n        \"node-0 with in-flight requests should have higher score: {} > {}\",\n        score0,\n        score1\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_rate_affects_score() {\n    let selector = test_selector();\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n\n    // Both have same latency initially\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Record errors for node-0\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_error(node0, Some(50.0)).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n\n    // With error_weight=10.0, node-0 score should be higher\n    let metrics0 = selector.get_metrics(node0).await.unwrap();\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node1).await.unwrap();\n\n    let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n    let score0 = metrics0.score(&config);\n    let score1 = metrics1.score(&config);\n\n    assert!(\n        score0 > score1,\n        \"node-0 with errors should have higher score: {} > {}\",\n        score0,\n        score1\n    );\n\n    // Verify error_rate is set\n    assert!(metrics0.error_rate > 0.0);\n    assert_eq!(metrics1.error_rate, 0.0);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_exploration_epsilon_configurable() {\n    // Test with epsilon = 0.0 (no exploration)\n    let selector_no_explore = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.0,\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 100,\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Make node-2 severely degraded\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node2, 500.0).await;\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // With no exploration, node-2 should get almost no traffic\n    let dist = simulate_selections(&selector_no_explore, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        count2, 0,\n        \"with epsilon=0.0, degraded node should get zero traffic\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_random_strategy() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        strategy: \"random\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Run many selections to test uniformity\n    let mut counts = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    for _ in 0..1000 {\n        if let Some(node) = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await {\n            *counts.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Each node should get roughly 1/3 of selections\n    let expected = 1000 / 3;\n    for (node, count) in &counts {\n        let diff = (*count as isize - expected as isize).abs();\n        assert!(\n            diff <= 100, // Allow 10% variance\n            \"{}: got {} selections, expected ~{}\",\n            node, count, expected\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":470,"startLine":1,"totalLines":470}}}

Env override: `MIROIR_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub master_key: String,\n    /// Key Miroir uses on Meilisearch nodes. Env override: `MIROIR_NODE_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub node_master_key: String,\n\n    // --- Core topology ---\n    /// Total number of logical shards.\n    pub shards: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group replicas per shard). Production: 2.\n    pub replication_factor: u32,\n    /// Number of independent query pools. health: HealthConfig,\n    pub scatter: ScatterConfig,\n    pub rebalancer: RebalancerConfig,\n    pub server: ServerConfig,\n    pub connection_pool_per_node: ConnectionPoolConfig,\n    pub advanced::ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n    pub query_planner: advanced::QueryPlannerConfig,\n    pub settings_broadcast: advanced::SettingsBroadcastConfig,\n    pub settings_drift_check:    pub peer_discovery: PeerDiscoveryConfig,\n    pub leader_election: LeaderElectionConfig,\n    pub hpa: HpaConfig,\n}\n\n/// Convenience alias.\npub type Config = MiroirConfig;\n\nimpl Default for MiroirConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            master_key: String::new(),\n            node_master_key: String::new(),\n            shards: 64,\n cross-field constraints. Returns `Ok(())` or a `ConfigError`.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {\n        validate::validate(self)\n    }\n\n    /// Layered loading: file → env overrides → CLI overrides.\n    pub fn load() -> Result<Self, ConfigError> {\n        load::load()\n    }\n\n    /// Load from a specific file path with env-var overrides applied.\n    pub fn load_from(path:          request_timeout_ms: default_request_timeout_ms(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP/2 connection pool per-node settings (§14.8).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, /// TTL for completed tasks in seconds. Tasks older than this are pruned.\n    pub ttl_seconds: u64,\n    /// How often the pruner runs, in seconds.\n    pub prune_interval_s: u64,\n    /// Maximum rows deleted per pruner iteration.\n    pub prune_batch_size: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Default for TaskRegistryConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache_size: 10000,\n            redis_pool_max: 50,\n            ttl_seconds: 7 * 24 * 3600, // 7 days\n            prune_interval_s: 300,       // 5 min\n            prune_batch_size: 10000,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Peer discovery via Kubernetes headless lease_ttl_s: 10,\n            renew_interval_s: 3,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Horizontal Pod Autoscaler settings (Helm-only, informational in config).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct HpaConfig {\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n}\n\n/// Policy for handling unavailable shards during scatter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    /// Return partial results from available nodes.\n    Partial,\n    /// Fail the request if any shard is unavailable.\n    Error,\n    /// Fall back to another replica group for unavailable shards.\n    Fallback,\n}\n\nimpl Default for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::Partial\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Partial => write!(f, \"partial\"),\n            Self::Error => write!(f, \"error\"),\n            Self::Fallback => write!(f, \"fallback\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Returns a minimal valid dev config (single-node, sqlite, RF=1).\n    fn dev_config() -> MiroirConfig {\n        MiroirConfig {\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            task_store: TaskStoreConfig {\n                backend: \"sqlite\".into(),\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            cdc: advanced::CdcConfig {\n                buffer: advanced::CdcBufferConfig {\n                    overflow: \"drop\".into(),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            search_ui: advanced::SearchUiConfig {\n                rate_limit: advanced::SearchUiRateLimitConfig {\n                    backend: \"local\".into(),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn default_config_is_valid() {\n        let cfg = MiroirConfig::default();\n        // Default has replication_factor=2 with sqlite, which should fail\n        // validation — but the struct itself should construct fine.\n        assert_eq!(cfg.shards, 64);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.replication_factor, 2);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.replica_groups, 1);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.task_store.backend, \"sqlite\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn minimal_yaml_deserializes() {\n        let yaml = r#\"\nshards: 32\nreplication_factor: 1\nnodes: []\n\"#;\n        let cfg: MiroirConfig = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).expect(\"deserialize\");\n        assert_eq!(cfg.shards, 32);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.replication_factor, 1);\n        // All §13 blocks should get defaults\n        assert!(cfg.resharding.enabled);\n        assert!(cfg.hedging.enabled);\n        assert!(cfg.anti_entropy.enabled);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn full_plan_example_deserializes() {\n        let yaml = r#\"\nmaster_key: \"test-key\"\nnode_master_key: \"node-key\"\nshards: 64\nreplication_factor: 2\nreplica_groups: 2\ntask_store:\n  backend: redis\n  url: resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n75\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n76\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n77\t            }\n78\t        }\n79\t\n80\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n81\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n82\t        } else {\n83\t            0.0\n84\t        };\n85\t\n86\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n87\t        let terms = term_df\n88\t            .into_iter()\n89\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n90\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n91\t                    0.0\n92\t                } else {\n93\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n94\t                };\n95\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n96\t            })\n97\t            .collect();\n98\t\n99\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n100\t    }\n101\t}\n102\t\n103\t// SearchRequest {\n341\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n342\t    ///\n343\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n344\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n345\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n346\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n347\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n348\t\n349\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n350\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n351\t\n352\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n353\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n354\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n355\t\n356\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n357\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n358\t\n359\t        // Ensure query is set\n360\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n361\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n362\t        }\n363\t\n364\t set if provided\n370\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n371\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n372\t        }\n373\t\n374\t        body\n375\t    }\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t#[derive(Debug)]\n379\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n380\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n381\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n382\t    pub partial: &Topology,\n389\t    query_seq: u64,\n390\t    rf: usize,\n391\t    shard_count: u32,\n392\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n393\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n394\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n395\t\n396\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n397\t        Some(g) => g,\n398\t        None => {\n399\t            return ScatterPlan {\n400\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n401\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n402\t            };\n403\t        }\n404\t    };\n405\t\n406\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n407\t\n408\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n409\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n410\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n411\t\n412\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n413\t            match selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n414\t                Some(node) => node,\n415\t                None => replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone(),\n416\t            }\n417\t        } else {\n418\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n419\t        };\n420\t\n421\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n422\t    }\n423\t\n424\t    ScatterPlan {\n425\t        chosen_group,\n426\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n427\t        shard_to_node,\n428\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n429\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n430\t    }\n431\t}\n432\t\n433\t/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n434\t///\n435\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n436\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n437\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n438\t    topology: &Topology,\n439\t    query_seq: u64,\n440\t    rf: usize,\n441\t    shard_count: u32,\n442\t    index: &str,\n443\t    floor: u64,\n444\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n445\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n446\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n447\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n448\t\n449\t    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n450\t\n451\t    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n452\t        shard_count,\n453\t        group,\n454\t        rf,\n455\t        query_seq,\n456\t        index,\n457\t        floor,\n458\t        version_checker,\n459\t    )?;\n460\t\n461\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n462\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n463\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n464\t        // Filter by version floor\n465\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n466\t            .iter()\n467\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n468\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n469\t                version >= floor\n470\t            })\n471\t            .collect();\n472\t\n473\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n474\t            return None;\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n478\t            // Convert Vec<&NodeId> to Vec<NodeId> for selector\n479\t            let eligible_owned: Vec<NodeId> = eligible.iter().map(|&n| n.clone()).collect();\n480\t            match selector.select(&eligible_owned, chosen_group).await {\n481\t                Some(node) => node,\n482\t                None => eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone(),\n483\t            }\n484\t        } else {\n485\t            eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone()\n486\t        };\n487\t\n488\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n492\t        chosen_group,\n493\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n494\t        shard_to_node,\n495\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n496\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n497\t    })\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n501\t///\n502\t/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n503\t/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\n504\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n505\t    topology: &Topology,\n506\t    query_seq: u64,\n507\t    rf: usize,\n508\t    shard_count: u32,\n509\t    pinned_group: u32,\n510\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n511\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n512\t    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n513\t\n514\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n515\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n516\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n517\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n518\t            continue;\n519\t        }\n520\t\n521\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n522\t            match selector.select(&replicas, pinned_group).await {\n523\t                Some(node) => node,\n524\t                None => replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone(),\n525\t            }\n526\t        } else {\n527\t            replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone()\n528\t        };\n529\t\n530\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n531\t    }\n532\t\n533\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n534\t        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n535\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n536\t        shard_to_node,\n537\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n538\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n539\t    })\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t/// Plan search scatter using adaptive replica selection (plan §13.3).\n543\t///\n544\t/// Uses EWMA-based scoring to select the best replica for each shard,\n545\t/// falling back to round-robin for shards with no metrics data.\n546\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n547\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_adaptive(\n548\t    topology: &Topology,\n549\t    query_seq: u64,\n550\t    rf: usize,\n551\t    shard_count: u32,\n552\t    replica_selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n553\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n554\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n555\t\n556\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n557\t        Some(g) => g,\n558\t        None => {\n559\t            return ScatterPlan {\n560\t                chosen_group,\n561\t                target_shards: Vec::new(),\n562\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(),\n563\t                deadline_ms: 0,\n564\t                hedging_eligible: false,\n565\t            };\n566\t        }\n567\t    };\n568\t\n569\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n570\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n571\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n572\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n573\t            continue;\n574\t        }\n575\t\n576\t        // Use adaptive selection to pick the best replica\n577\t        let selected = match replica_selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n578\t            Some(node) => node,\n579\t            None => {\n580\t                // Fallback to round-robin if selector returns None\n581\t                replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n582\t            }\n583\t        };\n584\t\n585\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    ScatterPlan {\n589\t        chosen_group,\n590\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n591\t        shard_to_node,\n592\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n593\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n594\t    }\n595\t}\n596\t\n597\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n598\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n599\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n600\t    client: &C,\n601\t    req: SearchRequest,\n602\t    topology: &Topology,\n603\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n604\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n605\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n606\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n607\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n608\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n609\t        }\n610\t    }\n611\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n612\t\n613\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n614\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n615\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n616\t\n617\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n618\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n619\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n620\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n621\t            None => {\n622\t                for shard_id in shards {\n623\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n624\t                }\n625\t                continue;\n626\t            }\n627\t        };\n628\t        let client_ref = client;\n629\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n630\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n631\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n632\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n633\t        let span = info_span!(\n634\t            \"scatter_node\",\n635\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n636\t            address = %node.address,\n637\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n638\t        );\n639\t        tasks.push(async move {\n640\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n641\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n642\t        }.instrument(span));\n643\t    }\n644\t\n645\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n646\t\n647\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n648\t        match result {\n649\t            Ok(body) => {\n650\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n651\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n652\t                }\n653\t            }\n654\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n655\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n656\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n657\t            }\n658\t            Err(e) => {\n659\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n660\t            }\n661\t        }\n662\t    }\n663\t\n664\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n665\t\n666\t    match policy {\n667\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n668\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n669\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n670\t            }\n671\t        }\n672\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n673\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n674\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n675\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n676\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n677\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n678\t\n679\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n680\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n681\t\n682\t                    // Try each other replica group\n683\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n684\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n685\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n686\t                        }\n687\t\n688\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n689\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n690\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n691\t                                continue;\n692\t                            }\n693\t\n694\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n695\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n696\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n697\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n698\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n699\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n700\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n701\t                                            break;\n702\t                                        }\n703\t                                        Err(_) => {\n704\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n705\t                                        }\n706\t                                    }\n707\t                                }\n708\t                            }\n709\t\n710\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n711\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n712\t                            }\n713\t                        }\n714\t                    }\n715\t\n716\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n717\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n718\t                    }\n719\t                }\n720\t\n721\t                // Merge fallback results\n722\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n723\t\n724\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n725\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n726\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n727\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n728\t                        shard_pages,\n729\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n730\t                        partial: false,\n731\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n732\t                    });\n733\t                } else {\n734\t                    // Some shards still failed\n735\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n736\t                }\n737\t            }\n738\t        }\n739\t    }\n740\t\n741\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n745\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n746\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n747\t    client: &C,\n748\t    req: SearchRequest,\n749\t    topology: &Topology,\n750\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n751\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n752\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n753\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n754\t\n755\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n756\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n757\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n758\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n759\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n760\t            });\n761\t        }\n762\t    }\n763\t\n764\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n765\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n766\t\n767\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n768\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n769\t        offset: req.offset,\n770\t        limit: req.limit,\n771\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n772\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n773\t        failed_shards,\n774\t    };\n775\t\n776\t    // Span for the merge operation\n777\t    let _span = info_span!(\n778\t        \"merge\",\n779\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n780\t        offset = req.offset,\n781\t        limit = req.limit,\n782\t    ).entered();\n783\t\n784\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n785\t}\n786\t\n787\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n788\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n789\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n790\t\n791\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n792\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n793\t    match query {\n794\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n795\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n796\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n797\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n798\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n799\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n800\t            }\n801\t            terms\n802\t        }\n803\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n804\t    }\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n808\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n809\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n810\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n811\t    client: &C,\n812\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n813\t    topology: &Topology,\n814\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n815\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n816\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n820\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n821\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n822\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n823\t        }\n824\t    }\n825\t\n826\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n827\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n828\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n829\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n830\t            None => continue,\n831\t        };\n832\t        let client_ref = client;\n833\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n834\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n835\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n836\t    }\n837\t\n838\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n839\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n840\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n841\t}\n842\t\n843\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n844\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n845\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n846\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n847\t    client: &C,\n848\t    req: SearchRequest,\n849\t    topology: &Topology,\n850\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n851\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n852\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n853\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n854\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n855\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n856\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n857\t    };\n858\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n859\t    let mut search_req = req;\n860\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n861\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Mock client\n866\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n867\t\n868\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n869\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n870\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n871\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n872\t    pub write_responses: HashMap<NodeId, WriteResponse>,\n873\t    pub fetch_responses: HashMap<NodeId, FetchDocumentsResponse>,\n874\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n875\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n876\t}\n877\t\n878\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n879\t    async fn search_node(\n880\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n881\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n882\t        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n883\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n884\t        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n885\t            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n886\t        }))\n887\t    }\n888\t\n889\t    async fn preflight_node(\n890\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n891\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n892\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n893\t        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n894\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n895\t        }))\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    async fn write_documents(\n899\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n900\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n901\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n902\t        Ok(self.write_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n903\t            WriteResponse {\n904\t                success: true,\n905\t                task_uid: Some(1),\n906\t                message: None,\n907\t                code: None,\n908\t                error_type: None,\n909\t            }\n910\t        }))\n911\t    }\n912\t\n913\t    async fn delete_documents(\n914\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n915\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n916\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n917\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n918\t            success: true,\n919\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n920\t            message: None,\n921\t            code: None,\n922\t            error_type: None,\n923\t        })\n924\t    }\n925\t\n926\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n927\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n928\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n929\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n930\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n931\t            success: true,\n932\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n933\t            message: None,\n934\t            code: None,\n935\t            error_type: None,\n936\t        })\n937\t    }\n938\t\n939\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n940\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n941\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n942\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n943\t        // Return stored fetch response if available, otherwise return empty\n944\t        let stored = self.fetch_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n945\t            FetchDocumentsResponse {\n946\t                results: Vec::new(),\n947\t                limit: request.limit,\n948\t                offset: request.offset,\n949\t                total: 0,\n950\t            }\n951\t        });\n952\t\n953\t        // Handle pagination: if offset exceeds total, return empty results\n954\t        if request.offset as u64 >= stored.total {\n955\t            return Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n956\t                results: Vec::new(),\n957\t                limit: request.limit,\n958\t                offset: request.offset,\n959\t                total: stored.total,\n960\t            });\n961\t        }\n962\t\n963\t        Ok(stored)\n964\t    }\n965\t\n966\t    fn get_task_status(\n967\t        &self,\n968\t        node: &NodeId,\n969\t        _address: &str,\n970\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n971\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n972\t        let node = node.clone();\n973\t        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n974\t        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n975\t        async move {\n976\t            if let Some(err) = error {\n977\t                return Err(err);\n978\t            }\n979\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n980\t                task_uid,\n981\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n982\t                error: None,\n983\t                error_type: None,\n984\t            })\n985\t        }\n986\t    }\n987\t}\n988\t\n989\t#[cfg(test)]\n990\tmod tests {\n991\t    use super::*;\n992\t    use crate::topology::{Node, c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n1165\t
