 }\n1174\t\n1175\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n1176\t    ///\n1177\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n1178\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n1179\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n1180\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n1181\t    ///\n1182\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n1183\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1184\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1185\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n1186\t    ///\n1187\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n1188\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n1189\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1190\t    ///\n1191\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n1192\t    #[tokio::test]\n1193\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1194\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1195\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1196\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1197\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1198\t\n1199\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n1200\t\n1201\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n1202\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1203\t\n1204\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n1205\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1206\t            total_docs: 1000,\n1207\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1208\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1209\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1210\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1211\t            ]),\n1212\t        });\n1213\t\n1214\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1215\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1216\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n1217\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1218\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1219\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n1220\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n1221\t            ]),\n1222\t        });\n1223\t\n1224\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n1225\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1226\t            total_docs: 10,\n1227\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n1228\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1229\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1230\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1231\t            ]),\n1232\t        });\n1233\t\n1234\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n1235\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1236\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1237\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n1238\t            filter: None,\n1239\t        };\n1240\t\n1241\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1242\t\n1243\t        // Verify global aggregation\n1244\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n1245\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n1246\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n1247\t\n1248\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n1249\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n1250\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1251\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1252\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n1253\t\n1254\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1255\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1256\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1257\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1258\t\n1259\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1260\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1261\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1262\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n1263\t        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1264\t        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1265\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n1266\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n1267\t    }\n1268\t\n1269\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n1270\t    #[tokio::test]\n1271\t    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n1272\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1273\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n1274\t that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n1328\t    #[test]\n1329\t    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n1330\t        let req = SearchRequest {\n1331\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1332\t            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n1333\t            offset: 10,\n1334\t            limit: 20,\n1335\t            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n1336\t            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n1337\t            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n1338\t            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n1339\t            global_idf: None,\n1340\t        };\n1341\t\n1342\t        let body = req.to_node_body();\n1343\t\n1344\t        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n1345\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n1346\t\n1347\t        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n1348\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n1349\t\n1350\t        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n1351\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n1352\t\n1353\t        // query must be set\n1354\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n1355\t\n1356\t        // filter must be set\n1357\t        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = Scatter orchestration: fan-out logic and covering set builder.\n\nuse crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector;\nuse tracing::{instrument, info_span, Instrument};\nuse crate::merger::{MergeInput, MergedSearchResult, MergeStrategy, ShardHitPage};\nuse crate::router::{covering_set, covering_set_with_version_floor, query_group};\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Scatter plan: the exact shard→node mapping for a search query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScatterPlan {\n    pub chosen_group: u32,\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n    pub shard_to_node: HashMap<u32, NodeId>,\n    pub deadline_ms: u32,\n    pub hedging_eligible: bool,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// §15 OP#4: Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch pattern)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n}\n\n/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightResponse {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalTermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n    pub idf: f64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalIdf {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n}\n\nimpl GlobalIdf {\n    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for resp in responses {\n            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n            total_length / total_docs as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let n = total_docs as f64;\n        let terms = term_df\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(term, df)| {\n                let idf = if df == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n                };\n                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Write path: document operations (P2.2)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Request to add/replace documents on a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    /// - None = client write (always emitted to CDC)\n    /// - Some(\"antientropy\") = anti-entropy repair write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"reshard_backfill\") = reshard backfill write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"rollover\") = ILM rollover write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"ttl_expire\") = TTL expiration delete (suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes)\n    ///\n    /// This field is NEVER stored on documents and never returned to clients.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteResponse {\n    pub success: bool,\n    pub task_uid: Option<u64>,\n    pub message: Option<String>,\n    pub code: Option<String>,\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete documents by IDs.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByIdsRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub ids: Vec<String>,\n    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete all documents matching a filter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByFilterRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub filter: Value,\n    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a delete operation.\npub type DeleteResponse = WriteResponse;\n\n/// Request to fetch documents with a filter (used for shard migration).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct FetchDocumentsRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub filter: Value,\n    pub limit: u32,\n    pub offset: u32,\n}\n\n/// Response from a fetch documents operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct FetchDocumentsResponse {\n    pub results: Vec<Value>,\n    pub limit: u32,\n    pub offset: u32,\n    pub total: u64,\n}\n\n/// Request to get task status from a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusRequest {\n    /// The task UID to query\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's task status query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// The task UID\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n    /// Current task status\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Error message if failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n    /// Error type if failed\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// Convert Meilisearch status string to NodeTaskStatus.\n    pub fn to_node_status(&self) -> crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n        match self.status.as_str() {\n            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\npub trait NodeClient: Send + Sync {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>;\n\n    /// Execute a preflight request (OP#4 global-IDF phase).\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(PreflightResponse { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() })\n    }\n\n    /// Write documents to a node (add/replace).\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Get task status from a node.\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        async move {\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n            results: Vec::new(),\n            limit: _request.limit,\n            offset: _request.offset,\n            total: 0,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum NodeError {\n    Timeout,\n    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n    NetworkError(String),\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SearchRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: Option<String>,\n    pub offset: usize,\n    pub limit: usize,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub ranking_score: bool,\n    pub body: Value,\n    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n}\n\nimpl SearchRequest {\n    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n    ///\n    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n    /// data to apply pagination.\n    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n\n        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n\n        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n\n        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n\n        // Ensure query is set\n        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n        }\n\n        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n        }\n\n        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n        }\n\n        body\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct ScatterResult {\n    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n    pub partial: bool,\n    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\npub async fn plan_search_scatter(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> ScatterPlan {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n        Some(g) => g,\n        None => {\n            return ScatterPlan {\n                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            match selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n///\n/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\npub async fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    index: &str,\n    floor: u64,\n    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n\n    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n        shard_count,\n        group,\n        rf,\n        query_seq,\n        index,\n        floor,\n        version_checker,\n    )?;\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        // Filter by version floor\n        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n                version >= floor\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if eligible.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            // Convert Vec<&NodeId> to Vec<NodeId> for selector\n            let eligible_owned: Vec<NodeId> = eligible.iter().map(|&n| n.clone()).collect();\n            match selector.select(&eligible_owned, chosen_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n    }\n\n    Some(ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n///\n/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\npub async fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    pinned_group: u32,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        if replicas.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            match selector.select(&replicas, pinned_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    Some(ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Plan search scatter using adaptive replica selection (plan §13.3).\n///\n/// Uses EWMA-based scoring to select the best replica for each shard,\n/// falling back to round-robin for shards with no metrics data.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\npub async fn plan_search_scatter_adaptive(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    replica_selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n) -> ScatterPlan {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n        Some(g) => g,\n        None => {\n            return ScatterPlan {\n                chosen_group,\n                target_shards: Vec::new(),\n                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(),\n                deadline_ms: 0,\n                hedging_eligible: false,\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        if replicas.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Use adaptive selection to pick the best replica\n        let selected = match replica_selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n            Some(node) => node,\n            None => {\n                // Fallback to round-robin if selector returns None\n                replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n            }\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    }\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\npub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n\n    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => {\n                for shard_id in shards {\n                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n        let shard_count = shards.len();\n        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n        let span = info_span!(\n            \"scatter_node\",\n            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n            address = %node.address,\n            shard_count = shard_count,\n        );\n        tasks.push(async move {\n            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n        }.instrument(span));\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n\n    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n        match result {\n            Ok(body) => {\n                for _shard_id in shards {\n                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n                }\n            }\n            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n                deadline_exceeded = true;\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n\n    match policy {\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n            }\n        }\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n\n                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n\n                    // Try each other replica group\n                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n                        }\n\n                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n                                continue;\n                            }\n\n                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n                            for node_id in replicas {\n                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                                        Ok(body) => {\n                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n                                            break;\n                                        }\n                                        Err(_) => {\n                                            continue; // Try next replica\n                                        }\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n\n                            if fallback_succeeded {\n                                break; // Found a working replica\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Merge fallback results\n                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n\n                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n                        shard_pages,\n                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n                        partial: false,\n                        deadline_exceeded,\n                    });\n                } else {\n                    // Some shards still failed\n                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n\n    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n    if scatter_result.partial {\n        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n\n    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n        offset: req.offset,\n        limit: req.limit,\n        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n        failed_shards,\n    };\n\n    // Span for the merge operation\n    let _span = info_span!(\n        \"merge\",\n        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n        offset = req.offset,\n        limit = req.limit,\n    ).entered();\n\n    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\npub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n    match query {\n        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n            }\n            terms\n        }\n        _ => Vec::new(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\npub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: &PreflightRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n    }\n\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => continue,\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let nid = node_id.clone();\n        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n}\n\n/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n    };\n    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n    let mut search_req = req;\n    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Mock client\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MockNodeClient {\n    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n    pub write_responses: HashMap<NodeId, WriteResponse>,\n    pub fetch_responses: HashMap<NodeId, FetchDocumentsResponse>,\n    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n    pub delay_ms: u64,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.write_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            WriteResponse {\n                success: true,\n                task_uid: Some(1),\n                message: None,\n                code: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            }\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        // Return stored fetch response if available, otherwise return empty\n        let stored = self.fetch_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            FetchDocumentsResponse {\n                results: Vec::new(),\n                limit: request.limit,\n                offset: request.offset,\n                total: 0,\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Handle pagination: if offset exceeds total, return empty results\n        if request.offset as u64 >= stored.total {\n            return Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n                results: Vec::new(),\n                limit: request.limit,\n                offset: request.offset,\n                total: stored.total,\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(stored)\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let node = node.clone();\n        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n        async move {\n            if let Some(err) = error {\n                return Err(err);\n            }\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn make_test_topology() -> Topology {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            let mut node = Node::new(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")), format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"), rg);\n            node.status = crate::topology::NodeStatus::Active;\n            topo.add_node(node);\n        }\n        topo\n    }\n\n    fn make_req() -> SearchRequest {\n        SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(), query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 0, limit: 10, filter: None, facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false, body: serde_json::json!({}), global_idf: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_pure_function() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n  }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_group_rotation() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        assert_eq!(plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await.chosen_group, 0);\n #[test]\n    fn test_extract_query_terms() {\n        assert_eq!(extract_query_terms(&Some(\"hello world hello\".into())), vec![\"hello\", \"world\"]);\n        assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let resp = vec![\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n        ];\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_empty() {\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&[]);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n        });\n        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n        });\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 80000);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"test\").unwrap().df, 8000);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: None,\n            offset: assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body handles large offset/limit correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_pagination() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 100,\n            limit: 50,\n            filter: None,\n test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response_1);\n\n        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, shards should succeed via group 1\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should have succeeded via fallback (group 1)\n        assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should have succeeded\");\n        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have shard pages from fallback\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when both groups are down.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_all_groups_down() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let self.parse_strategy();\n\n        match strategy {\n            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n\n        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n        if self.should_explore() {\n            self.observer.report_exploration();\n            let selected = self.select_random(candidates);\n            if let Some(ref node) = selected let Some(ref node) = selected {\n            self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), best_score);\n        }\n\n        selected\n    }\n\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    async fn P5.3 §13.3 Adaptive replica selection (EWMA-based) acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the EWMA-scored replica selection that replaces round-robin:\n//! - score(node) = α · latency_p95_ms + β · in_flight_count + γ · error_rate\n//! - Router picks lowest-scoring eligible node with probability 1 − ε\n//! - With ε (default 0.05) picks uniformly random for exploration\n\nuse miroir_core::replica_selection::{ReplicaSelectionConfig, ReplicaSelector};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\n/// Create a test selector with default config.\nfn test_selector() -> ReplicaSelector {\n    ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n}\n\n/// Create a selector with custom half-life for faster test convergence.\nfn fast_test_selector(half_life_ms: u64) -> ReplicaSelector {\n    ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        ewma_half_life_ms: half_life_ms,\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.05,\n        ..Default::default()\n    })\n}\n\n/// Create a test group with 3 nodes.\nfn test_group_3() -> Group {\n    let mut group = Group::new(0);\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".to_string()));\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string()));\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string()));\n    group\n}\n\n/// Simulate queries to measure node selection distribution.\nasync fn simulate_selections(\n    selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n    candidates: &[NodeId],\n    group_id: u32,\n    count: usize,\n) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> {\n    let mut distribution = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    for _ in 0..count {\n        if let Some(node) = selector.select(candidates, group_id).await {\n            *distribution.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n    }\n    distribution\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A1: Traffic shifts away from degraded node within 2× half-life\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a1_degraded_node_receives_less_traffic() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms half-life for fast test\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize all nodes with similar good latency (50ms)\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Run initial queries to establish baseline\n    let baseline = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n\n    // Verify roughly uniform distribution initially\n    let count0 = *baseline.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count1 = *baseline.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count2 = *baseline.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // Each node should get ~33% (allow 20-45% for randomness)\n    let _expected = 200 / 3;\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count0),\n        \"node-0 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count0\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count1),\n        \"node-1 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count1\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count2),\n        \"node-2 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count2\n    );\n\n    // Induce degradation on node-1: 200ms latency\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 200.0).await;\n    }\n\n    // Wait for EWMA to propagate (2× half-life = 200ms)\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run queries after degradation\n    let degraded = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n\n    let degraded_count1 = *degraded.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let degraded_count0 = *degraded.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let degraded_count2 = *degraded.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // Node-1 should receive significantly less traffic\n    // With latency_weight=1.0, node-1 score ~200 vs ~50 for others\n    // Expect node-1 to get <15% of traffic\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count1 < 30,\n        \"degraded node-1 still receiving too much traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count1\n    );\n\n    // Healthy nodes should receive more traffic\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count0 > 50,\n        \"healthy node-0 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count0\n    );\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count2 > 50,\n        \"healthy node-2 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count2\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A2: Node recovers after latency clears; distribution returns to uniform\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a2_degraded_node_recovers() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms half-life\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize with good latency\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Degrade node-1 with 200ms latency\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 200.0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Verify degradation worked\n    let degraded_dist = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 100).await;\n    let degraded_count1 = *degraded_dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count1 < 20,\n        \"node-1 should be degraded, got {} selections\",\n        degraded_count1\n    );\n\n    // Clear latency: record good responses for node-1\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    }\n\n    // Wait for recovery (2× half-life)\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run queries after recovery\n    let recovered = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 300).await;\n\n    let recovered_count0 = *recovered.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let recovered_count1 = *recovered.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let recovered_count2 = *recovered.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // All nodes should have roughly equal distribution (~1/3 each)\n    let expected = 300 / 3;\n    let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.4) as usize; // 40% tolerance for exploration\n\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count1 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-1 recovered count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count1, expected, tolerance\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count0 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count0, expected, tolerance\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count2 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-2 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count2, expected, tolerance\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A3: Exploration ensures degraded node still gets some traffic (ε = 0.05)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a3_exploration_samples_degraded_node() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 100,\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.05, // 5% exploration\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize: node-0 and node-1 healthy, node-2 severely degraded\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(node2, 500.0).await; // Very high latency\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run 1000 queries\n    let dist = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 1000).await;\n\n    let count0 = *dist.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count1 = *dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    println!(\"Distribution: node-0={}, node-1={}, node-2={}\", count0, count1, count2);\n\n    // Node-2 is severely degraded but should still get ~5% via exploration\n    // Expected: ~50 selections (5% of 1000), allow range 20-80 for randomness\n    assert!(\n        (20..=80).contains(&count2),\n        \"exploration not working: degraded node-2 got {} selections, expected ~50 (range 20-80)\",\n        count2\n    );\n\n    // Healthy nodes should split the remaining ~95%\n    let healthy_total = count0 + count1;\n    assert!(\n        healthy_total >= 900,\n        \"healthy nodes didn't get enough traffic: {}\",\n        healthy_total\n    );\n\n    // Each healthy node should get roughly half of remaining\n    let expected_healthy = 475; // ~95% / 2\n    let tolerance = 100;\n    assert!(\n        (count0 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n        count0, expected_healthy\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (count1 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n        \"node-1 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n        count1, expected_healthy\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A4: Round-robin fallback mode works identically to Phase 1 baseline\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a4_round_robin_fallback() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        strategy: \"round_robin\".to_string(),\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 5000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Test round-robin order\n    let first = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let second = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let third = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let fourth = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n\n    // Should cycle through candidates in order\n    assert_eq!(first, candidates.get(0).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(second, candidates.get(1).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(third, candidates.get(2).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(fourth, candidates.get(0).cloned()); // Wrap around\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional tests for EWMA behavior\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn ewma_converges_toward_new_values() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(50);\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let node = &group.nodes()[0];\n\n    // Start with 100ms latency\n    selector.record_success(node, 100.0).await;\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node).await;\n    assert!(metrics1.is_some());\n    let latency1 = metrics1.unwrap().latency_p95_ms;\n    assert!((90.0..=110.0).contains(&latency1));\n\n    // Shift to 50ms\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_success(node, 50.0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n\n    let metrics2 = selector.get_metrics(node).await;\n    let latency2 = metrics2.unwrap().latency_p95_ms;\n\n    // Should move toward 50\n    assert!(latency2 < latency1, \"EWMA should decrease toward new value\");\n    assert!(latency2 > 40.0, \"EWMA shouldn't overshoot too much\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn in_flight_count_affects_score() {\n    let selector = test_selector();\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n\n    // Both have same latency initially\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Add in-flight requests to node-0\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_request_start(node0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n\n    // With inflight_weight=2.0, node-0 score should be higher\n    let metrics0 = selector.get_metrics(node0).await.unwrap();\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node1).await.unwrap();\n\n    let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n    let score0 = metrics0.score(&config);\n    let score1 = metrics1.score(&config);\n\n    assert!(\n        score0 > score1,\n        \"node-0 with in-flight requests should have higher score: {} > {}\",\n        score0,\n        score1\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_rate_affects_score() {\n    let selector = test_selector();\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n\n    // Both have same latency initially\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Record errors for node-0\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_error(node0, Some(50.0)).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n\n    // With error_weight=10.0, node-0 score should be higher\n    let metrics0 = selector.get_metrics(node0).await.unwrap();\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node1).await.unwrap();\n\n    let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n    let score0 = metrics0.score(&config);\n    let score1 = metrics1.score(&config);\n\n    assert!(\n        score0 > score1,\n        \"node-0 with errors should have higher score: {} > {}\",\n        score0,\n        score1\n    );\n\n    // Verify error_rate is set\n    assert!(metrics0.error_rate > 0.0);\n    assert_eq!(metrics1.error_rate, 0.0);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_exploration_epsilon_configurable() {\n    // Test with epsilon = 0.0 (no exploration)\n    let selector_no_explore = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.0,\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 100,\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Make node-2 severely degraded\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node2, 500.0).await;\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // With no exploration, node-2 should get almost no traffic\n    let dist = simulate_selections(&selector_no_explore, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        count2, 0,\n        \"with epsilon=0.0, degraded node should get zero traffic\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_random_strategy() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        strategy: \"random\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Run many selections to test uniformity\n    let mut counts = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    for _ in 0..1000 {\n        if let Some(node) = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await {\n            *counts.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Each node should get roughly 1/3 of selections\n    let expected = 1000 / 3;\n    for (node, count) in &counts {\n        let diff = (*count as isize - expected as isize).abs();\n        assert!(\n            diff <= 100, // Allow 10% variance\n            \"{}: got {} selections, expected ~{}\",\n            node, count, expected\n        );\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":238,"oldLines":11,"newStart":238,"newLines":14,"lines":[" ","     println!(\"Distribution: node-0={}, node-1={}, node-2={}\", count0, count1, count2);"," ","-
